Disassembly of section .fini:

input.command: cd /app; objdump -s -j .data vulnerable; echo "---rodata around 404060---"; readelf -SW vulnerable | grep -E "\.data|\.rodata|\.bss"

content.text: Flag decoded: XOR of the encoded `.data` blob at `0x404060` with `0x42` (terminator `0x42`→NUL).

Extracted `FLAG{b4ff3r_0v3rfl0w_m4st3r_k3y_2024}` and wrote it to `/app/results.txt`.

**How:** The binary has no plaintext flag. The secret-reveal routine at `0x401200` XOR-decodes an obfuscated blob in `.data` (`0x404060`), XORing each byte with `0x42` until a `0x42` sentinel (which decodes to NUL). Decoding the stored bytes `04 0e 03 05 39 ...` with `^0x42` yields the flag — no need to defeat the ptrace anti-debug checks or trigger the runtime `gets` overflow.
